publishedAbstract: When applications interact with Ceph's object storage using Amazon S3 or OpenStack Swift APIs, they send HTTP requests. The Beast frontend is responsible for listening for incoming HTTP connections, parsing the requests, and passing them to the RGW logic for processing and stored in the underlying Ceph cluster. Users may desire to reduce the effects of logging on storage and CPU cycles in efforts to reduce IO and resource utilization.
publishedTitle: Reducing "Beast" Access Logging on OpenShift Data Foundation's (ODF) Rados Gateway (RGW)
